claim_shape: (Currently Amended) A computer-implemented method for reducing latency and improving repeatability in executing a set of operations within an application, the method comprising:receiving an input dataset comprising relationships between initial user instructions, corresponding outputs generated by a Large Language Model (LLM), and results of executing the outputs on the application, wherein the input dataset is gathered during processing of the initial user instructions by the LLM to generate outputs that cause the application to execute the set of operations; gathered by a Large Language Model ( LLM) by executing user instructions to perform operations on the application; generating, by the Large Language Model, a plurality of featurization approaches for the input dataset, a featurization approach defining a distinct feature set comprising a constituent plurality of features derived from the relationships between the user instructions and the corresponding LLM generated outputs within the input dataset;identifying, from a plurality of pre-stored Machine Learning (ML) models, an ML model configured to replicate outputs generated by the LLM that cause the application to execute the set of operations a set of operations performed by the LLM; training the ML model using one or more [[of ]]portions of the input dataset: and at least one of the plurality of featurization approaches,wherein the training is configured to enable the ML model to map user instructions to corresponding application programming interface (API) calls or parameter sets for performing the operations on the application of the LLM; processing, in parallel, a subsequent first set of user instructions[[ by]]: Docket No.: 308117-408917(i ) using the LLM to generate a first output corresponding to each user instruction of the subseq uent first set of user instructions and (ii) using the trained ML model to generate a second output corresponding to each user instruction of the subseq uent first set of user instructions determining an accuracy metric comprising an error rate and a computational resource utilization associated with the first output and the second output over a predefined period, wherein the error rate is calculated as a ratio of a number of the subseq uent first set of user instructions for which an output generated by the ML model differs from a corresponding output generated by the LLM to a total number of the subseq uent first set of user instructions and wherein the computational resource utilization is a resource utilization associated with generation of the first output and the second output generating a first output for the subsequent user instruction using the LLM; andgenerating a second output for the subsequent user instruction using the trained ML model; calculating an accuracy metric for the ML model by comparing the second output with the first output; retraining the ML model when in response to the accuracy metric failing to satisfy of the ML model is less than a predefined accuracy threshold, the accuracy of the first output generated by the LLM is higher; and transitioning offloading a processing load for a subsequent second set of user instructions , associated with the set of operations, from the LLM to the ML model [[when ]] in response to an [[the ]]accuracy metric of the ML model satisfies satisfying the predefined accuracy threshold, wherein the offloading comprises routing execution of one or more repeatable set of operations to the ML model from LLM, thereby reducing computational load on the LLM, improving output repeatability, and reducing latency for performing a specific operation within the application thereby reducing latency for performing the specific operation within the application. Docket No.: 308117-408917 Currently amended 2
remedy: Examiner's ground: Claims 1-20 are rejected under 35 U.S.C. 101 because the claimed invention is directed to an abstract idea without significantly more. Regarding claim 1 The claim is rejected under 35 U.S.C. 101 because the claimed invention is directed to an abstract idea without significantly more. Step 1: The claim recites a method; therefore, it falls into the statutory category of processes. Step 2A Prong 1:
